# Pull Request

## Description

A description of what is being added or changed. If bug, reference the bug link. Add screenshots/short videos where applicable

## Testing

What testing was done and what are the results. Add screenshots/short videos where applicable

## Accessibility Checklist

Tick every item that applies to this change. If an item does not apply, write `n/a`.

- [ ] All new images, diagrams, and screenshots have descriptive alt text (or are explicitly decorative with `alt=""`).
- [ ] Heading hierarchy in new or edited docs does not skip levels.
- [ ] New interactive elements in the docs site use native HTML (`<button>`, `<a>`, `<input>`) rather than clickable `<div>` or `<span>`.
- [ ] All form controls have a programmatically associated label.
- [ ] State changes (selected, pressed, expanded, current) are conveyed through `aria-pressed`, `aria-selected`, `aria-current`, or `aria-expanded`, not color alone.
- [ ] Focus is visible on every interactive element and tab order matches visual order.
- [ ] Any new animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion`.
- [ ] New error messages and log output state what happened and how to fix it, without relying on color.

## Reviewers

@mentions of 1 person at minimum to review the proposed changes
